name: iPayX
description: Provider of an MCP (modular connector) FX audit server and related payment/FX connector software (appears to publish connector code on GitHub rather than host public API docs).

auth_methods.note: No official vendor auth documentation was found on the vendor site. The primary developer-facing content is a GitHub repo for an MCP FX audit server which documents running/configuration but does not state a public API's supported auth flows (OAuth2, API keys, JWT, etc.).

credential_access.note: Vendor homepage contains no developer signup, dashboard, pricing, or credential instructions. GitHub repos show software to run but do not document a vendor-managed credential issuance flow, so it's unclear whether public API credentials exist or how they'd be obtained.

api_surface.note: No public API documentation or docs root was found on the vendor domain. The GitHub repo is the only developer-facing resource and contains code/readme for an MCP connector (suggesting code to run a local connector rather than a hosted REST/GraphQL API). This implies there is no broad vendor-hosted REST/GraphQL API documented publicly; the repo focuses on a specific MCP FX audit connector (narrow scope).

buildability.note: There are code repositories to run a connector locally, but there are no vendor-hosted public API docs or a clear way to obtain API credentials. This blocks building an integration against a public vendor API today. If the intent is to run the GitHub-provided server locally, that is possible, but that is not the same as using a documented vendor-hosted API.
